summary: Reviewed the implementation against the host-controller tweak spec, focusing on briefing trigger/rules gating, display parity, colour naming, and mobile spacing targets. The core host wiring is mostly present, but strict display parity is broken and the above-the-fold mobile guarantee remains unproven and likely under-budgeted. I also found one helper contract deviation and one data-shape ambiguity around prize stage labels.
appears_sound: The host briefing is gated by `currentGameState.numbers_called_count === 0`, and the normal stats/snowball strip is only rendered in the non-briefing branch in `src/app/host/[sessionId]/[gameId]/game-control.tsx:786`.
The rules sub-block is gated inside `PreGameBriefing` with `isFirstGameOfSession` in `src/components/host/pre-game-briefing.tsx:76`, matching the first-game-only requirement.
The call-delay hint has been removed and the nickname now renders above the ball with `slide-in-from-top-4` in `src/app/host/[sessionId]/[gameId]/game-control.tsx:797`.
The listed spacing diet classes in `game-control.tsx` match the SPEC table values: `p-5`, `mb-3`, `w-32 h-32 text-6xl`, `pt-3`, `mb-4`, `gap-3`, `h-20 text-2xl`, and `pb-24`.

findings.description: Implementation defect: the display rules refactor does not preserve the existing house-rules output byte-identically, which the spec makes a merge blocker. The shared rule text changes the first rule's apostrophe from the old JSX entity-rendered ASCII apostrophe to a curly apostrophe, and the mapped default text wrapper also risks rendering an empty class attribute where the original span had none.
findings.evidence: The old display JSX in the diff used `they're`, while `HOUSE_RULES` now contains `they’re` at `src/lib/house-rules.ts:28`; `display-ui.tsx` also passes `className={cn(rule.variant === 'closing' && 'font-bold italic')}` for default spans at `src/app/display/[sessionId]/display-ui.tsx:493`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: If the acceptance gate only checks broad screenshots, the empty class attribute may not be visible, but the text-node change is still a real DOM/copy change.

findings.description: Spec ambiguity / possible implementation defect: the spec says `stage_sequence` already contains display labels, but the included display UI has `formatStageLabel` for underscore/hyphen keys while the host briefing renders raw `stage`. If stored values are enum-like keys, the host will see labels like `two_lines` instead of `Two Lines`.
findings.evidence: `display-ui.tsx` defines `formatStageLabel` to normalize stage strings at `src/app/display/[sessionId]/display-ui.tsx:24`, while `PreGameBriefing` renders `Stage {i + 1}: {stage}` directly at `src/components/host/pre-game-briefing.tsx:52`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: Actual `games.stage_sequence` values may already be human-facing labels despite the display helper existing.
findings.what_would_confirm: Inspect the current `games.stage_sequence` data shape or generated type/migration; if values are keys, use an existing or shared stage-label formatter.

findings.description: Implementation defect: `getColourName` accepts hashless six-character hex strings, but the spec defines valid input as `#rrggbb` and says invalid input returns `Unknown colour`. This weakens the helper contract and can hide malformed colour data from the host.
findings.evidence: `HEX_RE` is `^#?([0-9a-fA-F]{6})$` at `src/lib/colour-name.ts:15`, and the test suite asserts `getColourName('ffffff') === 'White'` at `src/lib/colour-name.test.ts:16`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The broader parser may be intentional tolerance, but that behavior is not in the reviewed spec.

findings.description: Implementation defect: the first-game briefing layout likely misses the hard target that primary controls remain visible above the fold on a 390x664 iPhone Safari viewport. The scroll container uses a fixed `55vh` maximum while the controls are normal flow below the card, so the code does not reserve viewport space for header, card padding, margins, and the 156px control grid.
findings.evidence: `game-control.tsx` wraps the briefing in `max-h-[55vh] overflow-y-auto` at `src/app/host/[sessionId]/[gameId]/game-control.tsx:789`, then renders the control grid below it in normal flow at `src/app/host/[sessionId]/[gameId]/game-control.tsx:874`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: Actual dynamic viewport units and rendered content may still fit on the target device, so this needs a browser measurement.
findings.what_would_confirm: Capture the required iPhone 14 Pro Safari first-game briefing screenshot or compute the rendered layout height with the header and controls included.

findings.description: Verification gap: the spec explicitly requires screenshot gates for display rules parity and the three iPhone controller states, but the pack says those manual checks are still pending. This means two merge-blocking acceptance criteria are not yet satisfied even aside from the code-level parity issue.
findings.evidence: `display-refactor/handoff.md` says manual screenshot diff is still required, and `host-wiring/handoff.md` says manual screenshot checks remain pending at `tasks/host-controller-tweaks/.implement/wave-3/host-wiring/handoff.md:40`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: Screenshots may exist outside the pack, but the review pack itself records them as pending.
findings.what_would_confirm: Attach or reference passing before/after display diff and iPhone 14 Pro screenshots for in-play, game 2 briefing, and game 1 briefing.

empty_categories: No player follower route/component changes were made in the pack.
No data-model, server-action, or RLS changes were introduced by the five implementation files.
No gap found in the basic `numbers_called_count === 0` briefing trigger or `isFirstGameOfSession` rules-gating structure.
No static class mismatch found against the spacing diet table; remaining spacing risk is viewport-fit behavior and missing screenshot verification.
